Summarized the spec: a minimal-friction, tracking-first workout tracker where Markdown is the storage layer (not the UI), deviations are first-class, AI is a planning layer behind the product (not a chatbot), and the coach is configurable policy with no gamification.
how do you imagine the app? high level

Described a high-level vision: one live-in "Today" screen (90% of usage) showing the next set immediately, a lightweight plan/calendar that quietly repairs after gaps, and coach/context settings as plain-language configuration. AI appears as ghost targets, inline nudges (diffs, not chat), and a single "what should I do?" repair action.
make a wireframe runnable prototype use the multiple skills I added for you: ios-hig-design, mobile-ios-design, liquid-glass-design run it on my computere asap use sonnet agents to do the coding
Noted that only liquid-glass-design is installed; applied iOS HIG principles directly and loaded the liquid-glass skill, then handed the build to a Sonnet agent.

no, build it as an actual ios app -- I have xcode and everything! don't use card containers -- those are for the web and look bad on iphone -- bleed-edge instead. I do circuits or supersets very often so make that representable on the UI. the UI should be tik-tok like where I only see what set I need to do next and perhaps cues or something that can be attached by the coach for the exercise. I should be able to tap next or provide very easily feedback on how easy/hard it felt or write a note or whatever. open it on my ios simulator when ready
Built and launched the native iOS app on the simulator; captured a screenshot to verify rendering.
Tapped Start to enter the runner.

Confirmed the runner screen looks correct: full-bleed, floating glass top strip, giant hero target, coach cue pill, effort pills, reps stepper, and glass Note/Skip/Log & Next controls. Advanced into the superset block to verify the circuit mini-map.
Caught a mid-scroll frame but confirmed the superset page renders correctly with round indicator, mini-map, purple block hue, and coach cue. Grabbed a clean settled frame.

Diagnosed a paging bug: bottom controls via .safeAreaInset shrank the scroll container, causing partial overlap of adjacent pages. Fix in progress: full-screen ignoresSafeArea scroll container, full-height pages, floating overlay controls, and opaque page backgrounds for clean 1:1 full-screen snaps. Will rebuild and re-verify on simulator.
